summary: Every word character affects the required cursor movement, establishing a linear lower bound, while the fixed 26-key alphabet prevents lookup alternatives from creating a genuine slower asymptotic class.

optimality.argument: A correct algorithm must inspect each of the m word characters because changing any one character can change the total distance; the position map processes each character once.

replacement_checks.evidence: An unread word position can be changed to another key and alter the required movement total.
The reference builds a fixed 26-entry map and performs one constant-work update per word character.
Authored cases cover repeated keys, zero movement, both keyboard directions, and long words.
